focus: maintainability -- PASS B: UX gaps and automation/integration gaps

findingDetails.title: MK_GOAL_PLUGIN_DISABLED documentation overstates the disable boundary
findingDetails.claim: The documented disable flag says it disables goal injection and goal plugin behavior, but the implementation only suppresses passive injection and autonomous continuation while mk_goal tool mutations still execute.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked the live command router and plugin executor for an alternate disabled-mode guard; command routes directly to mk_goal/mk_goal_status and executeGoalAction has no options.enabled guard before mutations.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The intended contract may be to disable passive injection and autonomous continuation only while leaving manual tools active; if so, documentation should be narrowed.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if the intended public contract is explicitly injection/autonomy-only and ENV_REFERENCE is updated to say manual tool commands remain available.

findingDetails.title: /goal set output does not tell users whether it created, replaced, or refreshed a goal
findingDetails.recommendation: Add a terse mutation-result field such as mutation=created|replaced|refreshed and optionally previous_goal_id for replacements.

traceabilityChecks.spec_code: partial: MK_GOAL_* variables are documented, but MK_GOAL_PLUGIN_DISABLED wording overstates the actual tool disable boundary
traceabilityChecks.checklist_evidence: notApplicable: scoped phase folders are Level 1 packets without checklist.md
traceabilityChecks.automation: partial: existing hooks do not cover goal command/env/RICCE drift

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: non-trivial plugin command/env UX and automation surface

targetSelection.selectionReason: operator prompt explicitly scoped plugin, live command file, env docs, UX, and automation/integration gaps

searchLedger.hypothesis: Documented goal-plugin disable semantics do not match the implementation boundary.

searchActions.result: ENV_REFERENCE documents MK_GOAL_PLUGIN_DISABLED as disabling goal injection and goal plugin behavior.
options.enabled gates continuation and system transform, but executeGoalAction still runs tool mutations without an enabled guard.

searchLedger.rationale: operator-facing kill-switch wording overstates the actual disabled-mode behavior

searchLedger.hypothesis: The set command has no user-visible distinction between created, replaced, and refreshed goals.

searchActions.result: same-objective refresh and new/replacement goal creation both flow to goalStateLines(action=set).
status output has no mutation result field and command prints tool result unchanged.

searchLedger.invariant: Existing automation should be checked before minting another command/RICCE automation finding.

searchActions.result: Hooks cover comment hygiene, agent mirror sync, prompt-card sync, MCP mutation class, and tool ownership; no goal command/env/RICCE drift gate found.
No npm scripts are present for goal command/env/RICCE drift validation.

searchLedger.rationale: automation gap exists but command drift and RICCE drift are already active prior findings rather than a new class this iteration
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Covered by DR-009-P1-002 and DR-009-P1-003; new env disable mismatch is reported separately as DR-010-P1-001.
